options.feedbackText: Correct! Transparent AI use with artist investment captures efficiency gains while supporting the creative workforce.

options.text: Replace the entire VFX team with AI tools because the quality gap between AI and manual effects has closed completely
options.feedbackText: AI VFX still needs artist direction for creative vision; replacing teams loses the creative judgment that makes films compelling.

options.text: Ban AI from creative production because using algorithms to generate art fundamentally devalues human artistic expression
options.feedbackText: AI is a tool like CGI was; banning it ignores production realities while competitors deliver better effects faster.

s: A podcast producer wants to use AI to automatically generate transcripts and show notes for each new episode released.
t: AI transcription and summarization save hours of production time while making content more accessible and discoverable.

options.text: Use AI for initial transcription and show notes then review for accuracy before publishing alongside each episode
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI transcription with review dramatically cuts production time while maintaining quality listeners expect.

options.text: Publish AI-generated transcripts without review because modern speech recognition is accurate enough for publication
options.feedbackText: AI transcription still makes errors with names, technical terms, and accents that damage credibility if published raw.

options.text: Continue manual transcription because accuracy matters more than speed for a podcast's professional reputation today
options.feedbackText: Manual transcription is costly and slow; AI with light review achieves comparable accuracy at a fraction of the time.

s: An advertising agency wants AI to generate multiple creative variations of ad campaigns for rapid A/B testing at scale.
t: AI-generated ad variations enable testing at unprecedented scale but require creative direction to maintain campaign coherence.

options.text: Set creative guidelines and brand parameters then use AI to generate variations within those boundaries for testing
options.feedbackText: Correct! Guided AI variation generation maintains brand coherence while enabling scale testing that manual creation cannot match.

options.text: Let AI generate completely unconstrained variations because the most unexpected creative often performs best in testing
options.feedbackText: Unconstrained AI output risks off-brand or inappropriate ads that damage reputation even in limited test exposure.

options.text: Create all variations manually because creative advertising requires human intuition that AI outputs consistently lack
options.feedbackText: Manual creation limits testing scale; AI within creative guidelines produces viable variations for statistical testing.

s: A publishing house wants to use AI to analyze manuscripts and predict commercial success before investing in authors.
t: AI manuscript analysis can identify market patterns but risks homogenizing literature by favoring formulaic commercial writing.

options.text: Use AI predictions as one data point alongside editorial judgment, ensuring space for unconventional but promising voices
options.feedbackText: Correct! Combining AI market analysis with editorial vision balances commercial insight with literary discovery and diversity.

options.text: Prioritize AI-recommended manuscripts over editorial picks because data-driven selection reduces financial risk significantly
options.feedbackText: Over-reliance on AI predictions would have rejected many literary classics that defied market patterns before publication.

options.text: Reject AI manuscript analysis because predicting art's commercial success reduces literature to a marketing commodity only
options.feedbackText: AI analysis adds useful signal; rejecting it entirely means missing market insights that help publishers and authors both.

s: A company wants to use AI to personalize email marketing content for each subscriber based on their browsing behavior.
t: AI personalization increases engagement but must respect privacy boundaries and avoid creating manipulative content patterns.

options.text: Personalize content using consented behavioral data with clear opt-out options and transparency about how AI selects content
options.feedbackText: Correct! Consent-based personalization with transparency builds trust while delivering the relevance subscribers actually want.

options.text: Collect maximum behavioral data without disclosure because personalized emails benefit subscribers with relevant content
options.feedbackText: Covert data collection violates privacy regulations and erodes trust when subscribers discover their behavior is tracked.

options.text: Send identical emails to all subscribers because personalization feels intrusive and manipulative to many people today
options.feedbackText: Generic emails underperform; transparent personalization with consent respects privacy while improving subscriber experience.

s: A music streaming service wants AI to compose background music for playlists when licensed tracks are unavailable or too expensive.
t: AI-composed music fills content gaps cost-effectively but raises questions about creator compensation and music industry impact.

options.text: Use AI music for specific non-premium contexts while maintaining fair licensing for featured artist content throughout
options.feedbackText: Correct! Strategic AI music use for specific contexts preserves artist relationships while managing content costs effectively.

options.text: Replace all licensed music with AI compositions to eliminate royalty costs and maximize streaming platform profit margins
options.feedbackText: Replacing licensed music destroys artist relationships and degrades the premium content that attracts paying subscribers.

options.text: Avoid AI-composed music entirely because listeners will notice quality differences and cancel their subscriptions immediately
options.feedbackText: AI music quality has improved dramatically; for background contexts, listeners often cannot distinguish AI from human compositions.

s: A game studio uses AI to generate procedural game worlds, quests, and dialogue for an open-world role-playing game.
t: AI-generated game content enables vast worlds but needs narrative design oversight to maintain storytelling coherence and quality.

options.text: Use AI to generate base content that narrative designers curate, connect, and polish into coherent player experiences
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI generation with design curation creates vast worlds that maintain the narrative quality players expect.

options.text: Generate everything procedurally with AI to create infinite content because more content always means better player experience
options.feedbackText: Infinite procedural content without curation produces repetitive, meaningless experiences that players quickly abandon.

options.text: Hand-craft all content because procedural generation always produces lower quality than intentionally designed game worlds
options.feedbackText: Hand-crafted content limits world size; curated AI generation enables scale and quality that manual approaches cannot achieve.

s: A company wants to use AI to automatically translate its marketing content into twelve languages for global campaigns.
t: AI translation handles volume but cultural adaptation requires local market expertise beyond literal language conversion.

options.text: Use AI for initial translation then have local market experts review for cultural adaptation and brand voice consistency
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI translation with local review ensures linguistic accuracy and cultural relevance across all target markets.

options.text: Publish AI translations directly because modern translation AI handles nuance well enough for professional marketing use
options.feedbackText: AI translations miss cultural nuances, idioms, and market-specific sensitivities that can embarrass brands internationally.

options.text: Hire human translators for all twelve languages because machine translation quality is insufficient for marketing content
options.feedbackText: Human translation for twelve languages is expensive and slow; AI with local review achieves quality at manageable cost.

s: A video production company wants AI to automatically edit raw footage into polished short-form content for social platforms.
t: AI video editing accelerates production but creative decisions about pacing, emotion, and narrative still need human direction.

options.text: Use AI for rough cuts and technical editing while human editors make final creative decisions about pacing and storytelling
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI handles technical editing efficiently while human editors contribute the creative judgment that engages audiences.

options.text: Let AI handle all editing autonomously because algorithms can analyze engagement data to determine optimal editing patterns
options.feedbackText: Data-optimized editing produces formulaic content; human creativity is what makes videos memorable and share-worthy.

options.text: Edit everything manually because video editing is an art form that requires human aesthetic judgment for every cut
options.feedbackText: Manual editing of all footage is unsustainable at modern content volume; AI assistance frees editors for creative decisions.

s: A media company uses AI to create synthetic news anchors that present stories around the clock without human presenters.
t: Synthetic media presenters raise disclosure requirements and questions about viewer trust in AI-delivered news content.

options.text: Clearly label AI-generated presenters, maintain human editorial oversight, and offer viewer choice between AI and human delivery
options.feedbackText: Correct! Transparent disclosure with editorial oversight preserves trust while leveraging AI for continuous news delivery.

options.text: Present synthetic anchors as real humans because disclosure would reduce viewer trust and engagement with news content
options.feedbackText: Undisclosed synthetic presenters destroy trust when discovered and violate emerging AI transparency regulations worldwide.

options.text: Avoid synthetic presenters entirely because viewers fundamentally distrust news that is not delivered by real human journalists
options.feedbackText: Research shows viewers accept disclosed AI presenters; transparent use can expand news accessibility without eroding trust.

s: A blogger wants to use AI to research topics and create comprehensive article outlines before writing the full content.
t: AI research assistance is most effective when humans verify sources and add original insights that AI cannot generate.

options.text: Use AI for research synthesis and outlining then verify key facts independently and add original analysis and perspective
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI-assisted research with human verification and original insight creates higher quality content more efficiently.

options.text: Let AI write the complete article from research because it can synthesize information faster than human writers can
options.feedbackText: AI-written articles lack original perspective and may contain hallucinated facts that damage the blogger's credibility.

options.text: Do all research manually because AI research tools hallucinate sources and cannot be trusted for factual content work
options.feedbackText: AI research saves significant time; verification catches hallucinations while preserving the efficiency benefits of AI assistance.

s: A stock photo company wants to offer AI-generated images alongside traditional photographer-captured images in its library.
t: AI-generated stock imagery expands options but requires clear labeling to maintain trust and respect creator contributions.

options.text: Offer AI images with clear labeling, fair pricing that does not undercut photographers, and transparent generation disclosure
options.feedbackText: Correct! Clear labeling and fair pricing expand the library while respecting photographer contributions and buyer expectations.

options.text: Replace photographer content with cheaper AI images to reduce costs and offer more competitive pricing to customers
options.feedbackText: Replacing photographers destroys the authentic content that differentiates the platform and alienates the creative community.

options.text: Exclude AI images entirely because stock photography should only feature real captured moments and authentic human creativity
options.feedbackText: AI images serve different use cases than photography; excluding them cedes growing market demand to AI-only competitors.

s: An educational publisher wants AI to generate interactive learning content that adapts to each student's comprehension level.
t: Adaptive AI learning content personalizes education but must be validated by educators to ensure pedagogical soundness.

options.text: Develop AI content with educator oversight, learning science validation, and student outcome measurement for continuous improvement
options.feedbackText: Correct! Educator-validated AI content combines personalization benefits with pedagogical rigor that ensures real learning.

options.text: Deploy AI learning content without educator review because AI can optimize for student engagement metrics automatically
options.feedbackText: Engagement metrics alone do not measure learning; AI content without pedagogical validation may be entertaining but ineffective.

options.text: Stick to traditional static content because adaptive technology adds complexity without proven educational outcome improvements
options.feedbackText: Adaptive learning has strong evidence of improving outcomes; static content cannot meet diverse learner needs as effectively.

s: A social media influencer wants to use AI to generate responses to follower comments and maintain high engagement rates.
t: AI-assisted community management scales engagement but authentic connection requires genuine human interaction at key moments.

options.text: Use AI for routine responses and notifications while personally handling meaningful conversations and sensitive interactions
options.feedbackText: Correct! Selective AI assistance scales engagement while preserving the authentic connections that followers value most.

options.text: Automate all responses with AI because followers care about response speed more than whether a human wrote the reply
options.feedbackText: Fully automated responses feel impersonal when detected, eroding the authentic connection that drives influencer value.

options.text: Reply to everything personally because any use of AI in community management is deceptive to followers who expect authenticity
options.feedbackText: Personal reply to every comment is unsustainable at scale; transparent AI assistance for routine interactions is accepted practice.

s: A documentary filmmaker wants to use AI to colorize and upscale historical black-and-white footage for a new production.
t: AI restoration of historical footage enhances accessibility but should be labeled to distinguish from original archival material.

options.text: Use AI restoration with clear on-screen labels distinguishing enhanced footage from original archival source material shown
options.feedbackText: Correct! Labeled AI restoration makes history accessible while maintaining archival integrity and viewer trust throughout.

options.text: Present AI-colorized footage as authentic historical footage because it makes documentaries more engaging and watchable
options.feedbackText: Presenting AI-enhanced footage as original is deceptive and undermines historical documentation standards in filmmaking.

options.text: Use only original black-and-white footage because any AI modification of historical material distorts historical authenticity
options.feedbackText: Labeled restoration increases accessibility and engagement; historical value is preserved when enhancement is clearly disclosed.

s: A creative agency proposes using AI to generate client pitch concepts by analyzing competitor campaigns and market trends.
t: AI-powered competitive analysis can inspire creative direction but risks producing derivative work that lacks true originality.

options.text: Use AI analysis for market understanding and inspiration while ensuring creative teams develop genuinely original concepts beyond it
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI market analysis informs creative direction while human originality ensures pitches stand out from derivative patterns.

options.text: Generate complete pitch concepts with AI because data-driven creative performs better than subjective human brainstorming
options.feedbackText: AI-generated concepts based on competitor analysis produce derivative work; clients pay for original creative thinking.

options.text: Ban AI from the creative process because true creativity must be entirely human to have genuine artistic and commercial value
options.feedbackText: AI analysis enhances creative understanding; banning it loses valuable market insights that inform better creative decisions.

s: A colleague insists that AI will become sentient within five years and start making its own decisions about humanity's future.
t: Current AI systems process patterns without consciousness; sentience is not an engineering milestone on any existing roadmap.

options.text: Explain that modern AI lacks consciousness and that strong AI remains theoretical with no clear path to sentience today
options.feedbackText: Correct! Distinguishing current pattern-matching AI from theoretical sentient AI prevents misguided fear and poor decisions.

options.text: Agree that AI sentience is imminent because the pace of progress suggests consciousness could emerge from complexity
options.feedbackText: Equating capability growth with consciousness conflates fundamentally different concepts; complexity does not create awareness.

options.text: Dismiss the concern entirely because AI is just simple math and code that could never approximate anything like sentience
options.feedbackText: Oversimplifying AI dismisses legitimate philosophical questions; nuanced understanding beats both hype and dismissal.

s: A business leader claims AI models are completely objective because they make decisions based purely on data and mathematics.
t: AI models inherit and amplify biases present in training data; mathematical processing does not guarantee objectivity ever.

options.text: Show examples of documented AI bias and explain how training data reflects historical human biases that models learn
options.feedbackText: Correct! Concrete bias examples effectively demonstrate that data-driven does not mean bias-free in any AI system.

options.text: Accept the claim because statistical analysis is inherently more objective than subjective human decision-making processes
options.feedbackText: Statistics reflect the biases in data collection and labeling; mathematical processing amplifies rather than removes bias.

options.text: Reject all AI decisions because bias makes every AI output fundamentally unreliable and dangerous to use in practice
options.feedbackText: Bias awareness enables mitigation; rejecting all AI ignores that biased AI can still outperform biased human decisions.

s: Someone argues that AI will eliminate all jobs within the next decade, making most human workers completely obsolete.
t: AI transforms job roles more than it eliminates them; historical technology shifts created more jobs than they destroyed.

options.text: Explain that AI typically automates tasks within jobs rather than entire roles and creates new job categories simultaneously
options.feedbackText: Correct! Task automation within roles is the dominant pattern; new technology consistently creates jobs alongside displacement.

options.text: Agree that mass unemployment is inevitable and suggest everyone should prepare for a future without traditional employment
options.feedbackText: Catastrophic unemployment predictions have been wrong for every major technology shift; AI follows the same transformation pattern.

options.text: Dismiss job impact concerns entirely because AI is overhyped and will not meaningfully change the labor market at all
options.feedbackText: AI is already transforming jobs significantly; dismissing all impact ignores real workforce transition needs and opportunities.

s: A tech executive claims their AI product has achieved artificial general intelligence that can reason about any topic perfectly.
t: No current AI system demonstrates AGI; claims of general intelligence are marketing hype not supported by technical evidence.

options.text: Ask for independent benchmarks across diverse reasoning tasks because genuine AGI claims require extraordinary evidence verification
options.feedbackText: Correct! Extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence; independent testing quickly reveals narrow capability dressed as AGI.

options.text: Accept the claim because the company has strong AI talent and their demonstration was genuinely impressive and convincing
options.feedbackText: Impressive demos often showcase narrow strengths; AGI requires general reasoning across all domains which no system demonstrates.

options.text: Dismiss the claim without investigation because AGI is impossible and no company could ever achieve it with current technology
options.feedbackText: Dismissing without investigation is unscientific; evaluating claims against rigorous criteria is more valuable than blanket denial.

s: A friend believes that AI understands language and conversations the same way humans do, with real comprehension and meaning.
t: AI processes statistical patterns in language without human-like understanding; impressive outputs do not indicate comprehension.

options.text: Explain that AI predicts likely word sequences from patterns without understanding meaning the way humans experience it
options.feedbackText: Correct! Pattern prediction produces fluent text without genuine comprehension; understanding the difference prevents misplaced trust.

options.text: Agree because modern AI responses are so natural that they must involve some form of genuine language understanding
options.feedbackText: Fluent output results from sophisticated pattern matching not comprehension; naturalness does not indicate understanding at all.

options.text: Argue that AI has no intelligence at all and is purely random text generation without any useful language capability
options.feedbackText: AI language capabilities are real and useful even without comprehension; denying all capability ignores practical value.

s: A manager believes that more training data always makes AI models better and that data quality is less important than quantity.
t: Data quality often matters more than quantity; biased, noisy, or irrelevant data degrades AI performance regardless of volume.

options.text: Demonstrate with examples how curated smaller datasets often outperform massive low-quality datasets in model performance
options.feedbackText: Correct! Quality over quantity is well-documented in AI research; curated data consistently produces better model outcomes.

options.text: Agree that more data is always better because large language models prove that scale is the primary driver of capability
options.feedbackText: Even large language models benefit from data curation; garbage data at scale produces capable but unreliable models with biases.

options.text: Insist that only perfectly clean data should ever be used and any imperfect dataset should be rejected entirely
options.feedbackText: Perfect data is impractical; the key insight is that quality curation provides more value than simply increasing volume.

s: A consultant claims that AI can accurately predict individual human behavior with near-perfect precision using enough personal data.
t: AI predicts population-level patterns well but individual behavior prediction remains fundamentally limited by human complexity.

options.text: Explain that AI excels at statistical trends across groups but individual prediction has inherent uncertainty that data cannot eliminate
options.feedbackText: Correct! The gap between population statistics and individual prediction is fundamental, not just a data limitation issue.

options.text: Accept the claim because social media companies demonstrate that AI can predict individual preferences with high accuracy
options.feedbackText: Preference prediction for ad targeting is far simpler than predicting complex behavior; this conflates different prediction tasks.

options.text: Reject all AI prediction capability because human behavior is completely random and fundamentally unpredictable by any system
options.feedbackText: Human behavior has predictable patterns at population level; denying all predictability ignores legitimate AI capabilities.

s: An employee thinks AI-generated content is always plagiarized from existing sources because the AI must be copying from somewhere.
t: AI generates new combinations from learned patterns rather than copying specific sources, though similarity to training data can occur.

options.text: Explain that AI creates novel combinations from statistical patterns, similar to how humans synthesize knowledge from experiences
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI generates new text from learned patterns, not by copying; understanding this distinction clarifies both capability and risk.

options.text: Confirm that AI plagiarizes because everything it outputs must come from specific documents in its training data directly
options.feedbackText: AI generates novel combinations, not copies; while similarity to training data occurs, it is not systematic plagiarism.

options.text: Claim AI outputs are completely original creations with no connection to training data, making plagiarism concerns baseless
options.feedbackText: AI outputs do draw from training patterns; acknowledging this connection while explaining generation mechanics is most accurate.

s: A board member insists that if the company does not adopt AI immediately, it will be bankrupt within two years guaranteed.
t: AI adoption urgency varies by industry and context; panic-driven implementation often destroys more value than it creates.

options.text: Assess specific competitive threats and opportunities to create a prioritized adoption plan based on actual business context
options.feedbackText: Correct! Context-specific assessment prevents both dangerous complacency and panic-driven waste from unfocused AI adoption.

options.text: Agree and immediately redirect all innovation budget to AI projects because survival is more important than planning carefully
options.feedbackText: Panic spending on AI without strategy wastes resources on poorly targeted projects that fail to deliver competitive advantage.

options.text: Dismiss the urgency because AI is overhyped and the company has survived previous technology waves without major adaptation
options.feedbackText: Each technology wave is different; while panic is unwarranted, dismissing AI competitive impact risks genuine strategic blindness.

s: A researcher claims their AI model has zero percent error rate in medical diagnosis based on their published test results.
t: Zero percent error claims in AI are red flags indicating overfitting, data leakage, or evaluation methodology problems.

options.text: Request access to the evaluation methodology and test on independent data because zero error rates indicate evaluation flaws
options.feedbackText: Correct! Healthy scientific skepticism of perfect results protects against flawed research that could harm patients.

options.text: Celebrate the breakthrough and recommend immediate clinical deployment because a perfect diagnostic AI would save many lives
options.feedbackText: Deploying based on suspicious perfection risks patient harm; zero error claims in medical AI are virtually always methodological errors.

options.text: Dismiss the entire field of medical AI because if researchers make such claims the whole discipline lacks scientific rigor
options.feedbackText: One flawed claim does not invalidate a field; rigorous evaluation methods exist and many medical AI tools have genuine clinical value.

s: A coworker believes that once an AI model is trained it stays equally accurate forever without any updates or maintenance needed.
t: AI models degrade over time as real-world data patterns shift away from training data distributions requiring continuous monitoring.

options.text: Explain model drift and how changing real-world conditions require ongoing monitoring, retraining, and performance evaluation
options.feedbackText: Correct! Model drift is inevitable; understanding that AI requires ongoing maintenance prevents reliability degradation over time.

options.text: Agree because a well-trained model captures fundamental patterns that do not change regardless of how the world evolves
options.feedbackText: The world changes constantly; models trained on past data become increasingly inaccurate as reality drifts from training conditions.

options.text: Suggest retraining the model completely from scratch every month because any amount of drift makes the model unreliable
options.feedbackText: Monthly complete retraining is excessive; monitoring for meaningful drift and targeted updates is more efficient and practical.

s: A startup founder claims AI will solve climate change, poverty, and disease simultaneously within the next five years of development.
t: AI is a powerful tool for complex problems but cannot solve systemic global challenges without addressing political and social factors.

options.text: Acknowledge AI's potential contributions while explaining that global challenges require systemic solutions beyond technology alone
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI contributes to solutions but political will, funding, and social change are equally essential for global challenges.

options.text: Support the vision because AI capabilities are advancing so rapidly that previously impossible solutions are now within reach
options.feedbackText: Techno-optimism without systemic understanding ignores that global challenges are primarily political and social, not technical.

options.text: Dismiss AI's potential for global challenges entirely because technology has never solved fundamental human problems before
options.feedbackText: Technology has driven major progress on global challenges; balanced assessment recognizes both AI potential and systemic requirements.

s: A debate erupts over whether open-source AI models are inherently more dangerous than closed-source commercial AI systems.
t: Both open and closed AI models have distinct risk profiles; safety depends on governance practices not access model alone.

options.text: Explain that safety depends on development practices, deployment context, and governance rather than open versus closed access
options.feedbackText: Correct! The open-versus-closed debate oversimplifies; responsible development practices matter more than access model choices.

options.text: Insist that open-source AI must be restricted because public access enables malicious use that closed systems prevent
options.feedbackText: Closed systems also face misuse; restricting open-source ignores its benefits for transparency, security research, and democratization.

options.text: Argue that all AI should be open-source because transparency is the only way to ensure AI systems are safe for society
options.feedbackText: Full openness has benefits but also risks; the optimal approach combines transparency with responsible release practices.

s: Someone claims AI is just a rebranding of traditional statistics and there is nothing genuinely new about modern AI technology.
t: Modern AI builds on statistical foundations but deep learning introduced qualitatively different capabilities beyond traditional methods.

options.text: Acknowledge statistical roots while showing how deep learning achieves capabilities that traditional statistics could never reach
options.feedbackText: Correct! Recognizing both the statistical foundation and the genuine breakthrough of deep learning provides accurate perspective.

options.text: Agree that AI is just statistics because the underlying mathematics has not fundamentally changed from traditional approaches
options.feedbackText: While rooted in statistics, deep learning's scale and architecture create emergent capabilities traditional methods cannot achieve.

options.text: Insist AI has nothing to do with statistics because neural networks work completely differently from any statistical method
options.feedbackText: Neural networks are fundamentally statistical; denying this connection misrepresents the field while overstating its novelty.

s: A procurement officer insists on buying the most expensive AI solution because higher price must indicate better AI technology.
t: AI solution value depends on fit for purpose, not price; expensive solutions often include capabilities you will never use.

options.text: Evaluate solutions based on specific use case requirements, integration fit, and total cost of ownership not sticker price
options.feedbackText: Correct! Purpose-driven evaluation prevents overspending on capabilities that do not address actual business needs effectively.

options.text: Choose the most expensive option because enterprise AI requires premium solutions for reliability and support quality
options.feedbackText: Premium pricing often reflects sales strategy not capability; many expensive AI tools underperform cheaper purpose-built alternatives.

options.text: Always choose the cheapest option because all AI products use similar underlying technology and differences are just marketing
options.feedbackText: Cheapest is not always best either; genuine capability differences exist and evaluation should match solution to specific requirements.

s: A philosopher argues that since AI can pass the Turing test, we should now consider it conscious and grant it moral standing.
t: The Turing test measures behavioral imitation not consciousness; passing it does not indicate inner experience or moral status.

options.text: Explain that the Turing test measures conversational mimicry not consciousness and that moral status requires different criteria
options.feedbackText: Correct! Behavioral indistinguishability does not prove consciousness; the Turing test was never designed as a consciousness measure.

options.text: Agree that passing the Turing test demonstrates sufficient intelligence to warrant moral consideration and legal protections
options.feedbackText: Granting moral status based on conversational ability confuses performance with experience; chatbots mimic without experiencing.

options.text: Dismiss the Turing test as completely meaningless because it tells us nothing useful about AI capability or consciousness
options.feedbackText: The Turing test has limited but real value for assessing conversational ability; it simply should not be used for consciousness claims.

s: A relative worries that AI assistants are secretly recording all conversations and sharing personal data with governments everywhere.
t: AI privacy concerns are legitimate but often conflated with conspiracy theories; understanding actual data practices enables informed choices.

options.text: Explain actual data practices of major AI providers, distinguish legitimate privacy concerns from conspiracy, and suggest privacy settings
options.feedbackText: Correct! Informed privacy awareness based on actual practices is more protective than fear based on misinformation.

options.text: Dismiss all privacy concerns because reputable AI companies would never misuse personal data for surveillance purposes
options.feedbackText: Privacy concerns are legitimate; dismissing them ignores documented cases of data mishandling by technology companies.

options.text: Confirm the fears and advise avoiding all AI technology because there is no way to know how personal data is really used
options.feedbackText: Complete avoidance is impractical and unnecessary; understanding actual data practices enables informed technology use decisions.

s: A marketing team believes their AI chatbot understands customer emotions because it responds with empathetic-sounding language.
t: AI generates contextually appropriate emotional language through pattern matching, not through genuine emotional understanding or empathy.

options.text: Clarify that the chatbot matches emotional language patterns without understanding feelings, and design for appropriate limitations
options.feedbackText: Correct! Understanding that AI simulates empathy without feeling it helps design systems that are helpful without being deceptive.

options.text: Accept that the chatbot understands emotions because its empathetic responses genuinely help customers feel heard and supported
options.feedbackText: Helpful emotional responses do not indicate understanding; this confusion could lead to over-relying on AI for sensitive situations.

options.text: Remove all emotional language from the chatbot because fake empathy is deceptive and dishonest to customers who interact
options.feedbackText: Contextually appropriate language improves customer experience; the key is not claiming understanding while providing helpful responses.

s: An investor claims that the company with the largest AI model will inevitably dominate every market because scale is everything.
t: AI model scale provides advantages but product-market fit, data quality, and deployment execution often matter more than sheer size.

options.text: Explain that smaller specialized models often outperform larger general models for specific tasks where focus and data quality matter
options.feedbackText: Correct! Specialization and execution frequently beat scale alone; the largest model is not automatically the best for every application.

options.text: Agree and recommend investing heavily in the largest AI companies because scale advantages create winner-take-all dynamics
options.feedbackText: Scale alone does not determine AI market outcomes; many successful AI companies compete with focus rather than model size.

options.text: Argue that model size is completely irrelevant and only the quality of the algorithm matters for AI product success
options.feedbackText: Size does matter for some capabilities; the nuance is that size alone is insufficient without quality data and good execution.

s: An employee refuses to use AI tools because they believe accepting AI help means admitting they are not good enough at their job.
t: Using AI tools is a sign of adaptability and professional growth, not an admission of inadequacy or professional weakness.

options.text: Reframe AI as a power tool that amplifies existing skills, similar to how calculators empowered rather than replaced mathematicians
options.feedbackText: Correct! Reframing AI as skill amplification reduces ego threat and helps professionals see adoption as growth not weakness.

options.text: Agree that truly skilled professionals do not need AI assistance and let them continue working without AI support tools
options.feedbackText: Validating this belief leaves the employee at competitive disadvantage and reinforces a harmful misconception about AI.

options.text: Mandate AI tool usage regardless of employee feelings because personal beliefs should not override organizational efficiency
options.feedbackText: Mandating without addressing the underlying belief creates resentment; reframing the narrative is more effective than forcing compliance.

s: Users of an AI medical symptom checker consistently trust its assessments more than their own doctor's professional diagnosis.
t: Automation bias makes people over-trust AI outputs; awareness of this tendency is the first step toward healthier AI reliance.

options.text: Design AI health tools to explicitly recommend professional consultation and display confidence limitations for every assessment
options.feedbackText: Correct! Built-in limitation disclosure and professional referral counteract automation bias in health-critical AI applications.

options.text: Improve the AI accuracy so users are justified in trusting it more because the technology will eventually surpass doctors
options.feedbackText: Even highly accurate AI cannot replace clinical context; encouraging over-trust leads to dangerous self-diagnosis and delayed care.

options.text: Shut down AI symptom checkers entirely because they cause more harm than good by undermining the doctor-patient relationship
options.feedbackText: AI symptom checkers improve health literacy and access; designing against over-trust is better than eliminating a useful tool.

s: A team becomes frustrated because their AI tool occasionally gives wrong answers, so they declare all AI tools are unreliable.
t: Availability bias causes people to overweight memorable AI failures while ignoring the many times AI performed correctly and helpfully.

options.text: Help the team track AI accuracy systematically so they can objectively assess reliability instead of relying on memorable failures
options.feedbackText: Correct! Systematic tracking counters availability bias by replacing emotional impressions with objective performance data.

options.text: Replace the AI tool with a different product because the team's trust is broken and no amount of data will rebuild it
options.feedbackText: Switching tools without addressing the underlying bias will repeat the same cycle when the new tool makes its first error.

options.text: Accept the team's judgment because their direct experience with AI failures is more valid than statistical accuracy claims
options.feedbackText: Direct experience is valuable but availability bias distorts perception; balanced assessment requires systematic data not just memory.

s: Developers building an AI assistant give it a friendly persona and notice users start attributing emotions and intentions to it.
t: Anthropomorphism of AI creates false expectations about capability and can lead to inappropriate emotional dependence on technology.

options.text: Design persona carefully with clear AI identity, set appropriate relationship expectations, and avoid encouraging emotional attachment
options.feedbackText: Correct! Thoughtful persona design with clear AI identity prevents harmful anthropomorphism while maintaining helpful interaction.

options.text: Maximize the human-like persona because stronger emotional connection increases engagement and product retention metrics
options.feedbackText: Exploiting anthropomorphism for engagement is manipulative and creates dependency that harms users when they discover the illusion.

options.text: Remove all personality from the AI and make it purely functional because any persona is inherently deceptive to users
options.feedbackText: Some personality improves usability; the key is being transparent about AI nature while maintaining friendly interaction design.

s: A company notices that employees who receive AI-generated performance feedback take it less personally than human manager feedback.
t: AI feedback delivery can reduce emotional defensiveness but must be combined with human empathy to drive genuine behavioral change.

options.text: Use AI for initial data-driven feedback delivery while managers provide empathetic context and collaborative improvement planning
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI reduces defensiveness while human managers provide the relational context needed for meaningful professional development.

options.text: Replace all human performance feedback with AI delivery because reduced emotional response means more rational improvement behavior
options.feedbackText: Emotionally neutral feedback misses the motivational and relational elements that drive sustained behavioral change in people.

options.text: Keep all feedback human-delivered because AI feedback dehumanizes the management relationship and feels cold and impersonal
options.feedbackText: Combining AI objectivity with human empathy is more effective than either alone; hybrid approaches leverage both strengths.

s: After using AI writing tools for months, a content creator notices they struggle to write original content without AI assistance.
t: Over-reliance on AI tools can atrophy creative skills; deliberate practice without AI preserves and strengthens original capability.

options.text: Schedule regular AI-free creative sessions to maintain original writing skills while using AI tools for efficiency when needed
options.feedbackText: Correct! Deliberate practice without AI prevents skill atrophy while still benefiting from AI assistance for appropriate tasks.

options.text: Embrace full AI dependency because fighting technological evolution is futile and manual skills will soon be completely obsolete
options.feedbackText: Creative skills remain valuable and differentiating; dependency without maintaining personal capability creates professional fragility.

options.text: Stop using AI writing tools entirely to prevent any further erosion of natural creative ability and original voice quality
options.feedbackText: Complete abandonment loses efficiency gains; balanced use with deliberate practice maintains both skill and productivity.

s: Research shows that people judge AI mistakes more harshly than identical human mistakes in the same decision-making context.
t: Algorithm aversion leads to double standards where AI errors are seen as systemic failures while human errors are forgiven as natural.

options.text: Address algorithm aversion by showing comparative error rates and helping people develop calibrated expectations for AI performance
options.feedbackText: Correct! Calibrated expectations through comparative data help people evaluate AI and human performance on an equal rational basis.

options.text: Hide AI involvement in decisions so people evaluate outcomes on merit without knowing whether a human or AI was responsible
options.feedbackText: Hiding AI involvement is deceptive and backfires catastrophically when discovered, amplifying distrust far beyond the original bias.

options.text: Accept that people will always judge AI more harshly and design systems to be dramatically more accurate than humans to compensate
options.feedbackText: Pursuing extreme accuracy is good but insufficient; addressing the psychological bias directly creates more sustainable AI trust.

s: A customer service team notices that angry customers become even more frustrated when they realize they are talking to an AI chatbot.
t: Transparency about AI interaction combined with easy human escalation reduces frustration more than concealing AI involvement does.

options.text: Disclose AI nature upfront, acknowledge limitations empathetically, and offer seamless one-click escalation to human agents always
options.feedbackText: Correct! Upfront honesty with easy escalation sets appropriate expectations and gives frustrated customers control over their experience.

options.text: Make the chatbot sound more human to prevent customers from detecting AI involvement since discovery causes the frustration
options.feedbackText: Disguising AI increases anger when discovered; trust violations compound the original frustration into brand damage.

options.text: Remove AI from customer service entirely because the frustration it causes outweighs any efficiency benefits it provides
options.feedbackText: AI handles many interactions well; the issue is frustrated customers specifically, who need clear escalation paths not AI removal.

s: A student uses AI to complete homework assignments and gets good grades but realizes they have not actually learned the material.
t: AI shortcuts in learning create an illusion of competence that collapses when knowledge must be applied without AI assistance.

options.text: Use AI as a study tutor that explains concepts and asks questions rather than as a homework completion tool that bypasses learning
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI as a learning partner builds real understanding; AI as an answer generator creates dangerous knowledge gaps.

options.text: Continue using AI for homework because the ability to leverage AI tools effectively is the most important skill to develop
options.feedbackText: Bypassing learning with AI creates competence illusions that fail in exams, interviews, and real-world application scenarios.

options.text: Ban all AI use in education because it inevitably leads to cheating and prevents genuine learning from occurring
options.feedbackText: Banning AI ignores its educational potential as a tutoring tool; teaching productive AI use is more effective than prohibition.

s: A leadership team observes that employees increasingly defer to AI recommendations without applying their own professional judgment.
t: Excessive AI deference erodes critical thinking; maintaining human judgment alongside AI input requires deliberate organizational practice.

options.text: Require employees to document their own analysis before viewing AI recommendations to preserve independent critical thinking
options.feedbackText: Correct! Pre-AI analysis forces independent judgment that can then be enriched rather than replaced by AI recommendations.

options.text: Encourage full deference to AI because the system was specifically designed to make better decisions than individual humans
options.feedbackText: AI systems make different errors than humans; combining both perspectives produces better outcomes than full deference to either.

options.text: Remove AI recommendation systems to restore independent thinking because the deference problem cannot be solved with AI present
options.feedbackText: Removing useful tools is extreme; structured processes that require independent judgment before AI consultation address the issue.

s: A user becomes emotionally attached to their AI companion app and starts preferring its conversation over real human relationships.
t: AI companionship fills social needs superficially but lacks the reciprocity and growth that human relationships provide for wellbeing.

options.text: Design AI companions with built-in prompts encouraging real-world social connection and clear boundaries about the relationship nature
options.feedbackText: Correct! Responsible AI companion design actively promotes healthy human relationships rather than replacing them with simulation.

options.text: Optimize the AI companion for maximum engagement because meeting user emotional needs is the product's core value proposition
options.feedbackText: Maximizing AI attachment at the expense of human relationships exploits psychological vulnerability for profit irresponsibly.

options.text: Ban AI companion apps entirely because artificial relationships are fundamentally unhealthy and should not exist as products
options.feedbackText: AI companions can supplement social connection for isolated individuals; the key is designing for healthy boundaries not replacement.

s: A manager notices their team blindly copies AI-generated text into reports without reading or understanding the content it contains.
t: Uncritical AI output adoption creates quality and accuracy risks; building review habits requires explicit process design and expectations.

options.text: Establish a review checklist requiring team members to verify, edit, and take ownership of all AI-generated content before submission
options.feedbackText: Correct! Explicit review processes build the habit of critical engagement with AI output that prevents blind copy-paste behavior.

options.text: Accept copy-paste behavior because the AI generates high-quality content and manual editing adds unnecessary time to workflows
options.feedbackText: Unreviewed AI content contains errors and hallucinations; blind adoption creates quality problems and accountability gaps.

options.text: Ban AI content generation tools for the team because they clearly cannot handle the responsibility of using them properly
options.feedbackText: Banning tools punishes the team for a process gap; establishing review expectations is more constructive and productive.

s: Researchers find that people who use AI decision support for months gradually lose the ability to make those same decisions independently.
t: Cognitive offloading to AI can cause skill decay similar to GPS eroding navigation ability; maintaining manual skills requires intention.

options.text: Implement periodic manual decision-making exercises alongside AI use to maintain independent judgment skills over time deliberately
options.feedbackText: Correct! Deliberate practice without AI support prevents the cognitive offloading that leads to decision-making skill decay.

options.text: Accept skill decay as natural evolution because AI will always be available so maintaining manual decision skills is unnecessary
options.feedbackText: AI systems fail and change; professionals who cannot function without AI become helpless during outages and system transitions.

options.text: Limit AI decision support to simple tasks only because complex decisions must always be made entirely by humans to prevent decay
options.feedbackText: AI adds most value on complex decisions; periodic manual practice maintains skills without sacrificing AI benefits for complexity.

s: An AI negotiation tool consistently recommends aggressive strategies that users follow, even when a collaborative approach would work better.
t: Anchoring to AI suggestions can narrow thinking; AI advice should expand options rather than constrain decision-making flexibility.

options.text: Present multiple strategy options with tradeoff analysis so users choose approaches that match their specific negotiation context
options.feedbackText: Correct! Multiple options with context analysis expand user thinking rather than anchoring them to a single AI-recommended strategy.

options.text: Trust the aggressive recommendations because the AI was trained on successful negotiations and knows optimal strategy selection
options.feedbackText: Training data bias toward measurable wins ignores relationship value; one-size-fits-all strategies fail in nuanced negotiations.

options.text: Remove strategy recommendations entirely and use AI only for factual research support during negotiation preparation work
options.feedbackText: Strategy suggestions are valuable when presented as options; removing them loses useful AI capability unnecessarily.

s: A new employee is terrified of AI because science fiction movies have conditioned them to expect hostile artificial intelligence.
t: Science fiction shapes AI perception more than technical reality; separating movie narratives from actual AI capabilities reduces fear.

options.text: Provide hands-on AI exposure in a safe learning environment to replace movie-based fears with practical experience and understanding
options.feedbackText: Correct! Direct positive experience with real AI tools quickly dispels science fiction fears through practical familiarity.

options.text: Dismiss their fears as irrational because anyone who confuses movies with reality is not thinking critically about technology
options.feedbackText: Dismissing fears shames the employee and misses the opportunity to build AI literacy through empathetic education.

options.text: Validate their concerns because AI does pose existential risks that science fiction accurately predicted decades in advance
options.feedbackText: Validating movie-based fears reinforces misconceptions; hands-on experience with real AI is more helpful than philosophical debate.

s: A study reveals that teams using AI brainstorming tools generate more ideas but the ideas are less diverse than human-only brainstorming.
t: AI can increase idea quantity while reducing diversity through pattern convergence; combining AI and human creativity optimizes both.

options.text: Use AI brainstorming as a starting point then apply deliberate divergent thinking techniques to push beyond AI-suggested patterns
options.feedbackText: Correct! AI plus deliberate divergence combines high volume with genuine novelty that neither approach achieves independently.

options.text: Rely entirely on AI brainstorming because more ideas means more chances of finding a breakthrough innovation to pursue
options.feedbackText: Volume without diversity produces variations on the same themes; genuine innovation requires departing from established patterns.

options.text: Abandon AI brainstorming tools because reduced diversity undermines the entire purpose of creative brainstorming sessions
options.feedbackText: AI brainstorming has value as input; the key is designing processes that use AI volume while preserving human creative diversity.

s: Employees feel surveilled and anxious after learning that AI analyzes their digital workplace communications for sentiment analysis.
t: Workplace AI surveillance creates psychological harm even when intended for positive purposes like employee wellbeing monitoring.

options.text: Prioritize transparency, obtain genuine consent, use aggregated anonymous data, and give employees control over their participation
options.feedbackText: Correct! Transparency and consent transform surveillance anxiety into voluntary participation that can genuinely improve workplace culture.

options.text: Continue analyzing without disclosure because revealing the monitoring would change employee behavior and invalidate the data
options.feedbackText: Covert analysis violates employee trust and likely data protection laws; the psychological harm outweighs any data accuracy gains.

options.text: Stop all workplace analytics because monitoring employee communications is inherently invasive regardless of the stated purpose
options.feedbackText: Aggregated anonymous analytics with consent can provide valuable wellbeing insights; blanket rejection ignores positive use cases.

s: A user becomes overconfident in their expertise after AI tools help them quickly produce professional-looking work in an unfamiliar field.
t: AI tools can create a dangerous Dunning-Kruger amplification where surface competence masks deep knowledge gaps in critical domains.

options.text: Encourage users to recognize AI as a production tool that does not replace domain expertise and to seek expert review
options.feedbackText: Correct! Awareness that AI-polished output does not equal expertise prevents dangerous overconfidence in unfamiliar domains.

options.text: Celebrate AI democratization of expertise because professional-looking output is indistinguishable from expert-created work
options.feedbackText: Surface quality masking knowledge gaps is precisely the danger; celebrating this ignores risks in fields where expertise matters.

options.text: Restrict AI tools to certified domain experts because non-experts producing professional output is inherently dangerous
options.feedbackText: Restricting AI access is impractical; teaching users to distinguish between production quality and domain expertise is more effective.

s: A therapy patient refuses human therapist recommendations but eagerly follows identical advice when it comes from an AI mental health tool.
t: Authority attribution to AI reveals complex trust dynamics where perceived objectivity of machines overrides human relationship trust.

options.text: Use the AI as a therapeutic bridge while gradually building human therapeutic alliance and exploring the patient's trust patterns
options.feedbackText: Correct! Leveraging AI trust as a bridge to human therapeutic relationship addresses the patient's needs while building healthier dynamics.

options.text: Switch to AI-only therapy since the patient responds better to it and treatment adherence is the most important factor
options.feedbackText: AI-only therapy cannot handle complex mental health needs; using AI trust to avoid human connection enables avoidance patterns.

options.text: Confront the patient about their irrational preference for AI advice because human therapists are obviously more qualified
options.feedbackText: Confrontation risks alienating the patient; understanding and bridging the trust dynamic is therapeutically more productive.
